codeReview.summary: The implementation matches the feature requirements: tier-based weighted scoring constants, dynamic problem-to-tier mapping, weighted and raw outputs, per-category weighted scores, and broad test coverage (31 tests) for required behaviors and edge cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture docs appear stale for scoring semantics: architecture.md documents category-weighted composite scoring, while the implemented scoring module uses tier weights (easy/hard/expert/tool_calling).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:144-151 shows CATEGORY_WEIGHTS-based scoring; mtb/quality_benchmarks/scoring.py:22-26 defines TIER_WEIGHTS and compute_weighted_score uses tier weighting at lines 130-133.

sharedStateObservations.observation: python-worker skill procedure requires running tests/test_quality_benchmarks.py, but this feature handoff marks followedProcedure=true without that command in verification evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md:66 requires `uv run pytest tests/test_quality_benchmarks.py -v --tb=short`; handoff commands in mission handoff file lines 14-36 do not include it, while skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true at lines 178-180.

summary: Reviewed feature weighted-scoring-module at commit b84edc5. Code and tests satisfy expected behavior with no blocking or non-blocking defects found; report includes shared-state observations for architecture-doc drift and skill-procedure/evidence mismatch.
